recipe.title: Turkish Chicken Meatballs with Garlic Yogurt Sauce & Cabbage Rolls
steps: Mix 2½ lb ground chicken with breadcrumbs, grated onion, garlic, eggs, parsley, cumin, coriander, paprika, chili flakes, salt, and pepper.
Form into ~30 meatballs. Bake on a lined sheet at 425°F for 18–20 minutes until cooked through.
Meanwhile, bring 1½ cups chicken broth to a simmer. Whisk together Greek yogurt, eggs, and cornstarch slurry.
Slowly temper yogurt mixture into hot broth, stirring constantly. Cook until thickened. Season with salt and pepper.
Blanch cabbage leaves in boiling water for 3-4 minutes until pliable. Drain and cool.
Mix 2 lb ground chicken with cooked rice, drained diced tomatoes, onion, garlic, parsley, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Spoon filling onto each cabbage leaf and roll tightly, tucking in the edges.
Arrange cabbage rolls seam-side down in a large baking dish. Pour crushed tomatoes and 1 cup broth over rolls.
Cover with foil and bake at 350°F for 45–55 minutes until cabbage is tender and filling is cooked through.
Serve meatballs with garlic yogurt sauce drizzled with olive oil and Aleppo pepper. Serve cabbage rolls with extra tomato sauce.
